description: A J Vol7 Chickasha Indian Territory Monday Evening June 25 1906 NUMBER 149 Land Wanted DeeringReal Estate Co success to Scot 2Jones3Real Estate Co We have tenants for 1000 Acres of Land Parties who have lani to rent let us know at once We can rent your land for you See us now TAXES IN THE NEW STATE GUTHRIE BANKER FICURES THAT THEY WILL BE LOWER THAN AT PRESENT IN OKLAHOMA DANGER WILL LIE IN COUNTIES Great Care Will be Needed to Keep Counties From Running into Debt The Gutlirie correspondent for the Kansas City Star writes on tax ation in the New State as fol lows What will be the rate of taxation under statehood Will there be more taxes under statehood than territorial laws These questions are general throughout Oklahoma and Indian Territory and have given rise to many exaggerated and untruthful statements Persons who have given close study to present and future conditions in the two terri tories agree generally that the cost of maintaining a state government should not be much greater than the cost of government in the ter ritory of Oklahoma and that under statehood reasonable economy should jive a rate of taxation slight
